EI.dimName.5.en: Attitude toward meetings and public speaking

EI.E_81-100.dim.0.en: Responds before thinking, may contradict themselves in answers, and becomes irritated when others do not reply immediately.

EI.E_81-100.dim.1.en: Prefers spending time in the largest and noisiest groups possible, and often cannot remember the names of their many acquaintances and friends.

EI.E_81-100.dim.2.en: If a situation displeases them, they may flare up and will not calm down until the situation changes, drawing in as many people as possible to help effect that change.

EI.E_81-100.dim.3.en: Most people find them excessively uninhibited; they frequently violate others' personal boundaries.

EI.E_81-100.dim.4.en: Often interrupts others during conversations, can participate in several discussions simultaneously, and talk on the phone at the same time.

EI.E_81-100.dim.5.en: Cannot function without meetings; arrives first, always speaks up, frequently initiates gatherings themselves, and gains a powerful emotional charge from meetings, often at the expense of other participants.

EI.E_81-100.dim.6.en: Constantly shares their thoughts and ideas with everyone around them, often in an intrusive manner.

EI.E_81-100.dim.7.en: Cannot truly listen in conversation; interrupts and takes over, always acting as the initiator and center of any discussion.

EI.E_81-100.dim.8.en: Constantly talks to themselves, frequently refers to themselves in the third person, thinks out loud, and even reads aloud.

EI.E_81-100.dim.9.en: Demands public approval of their actions, seeking praise, admiration, compliments, and even outright flattery.

EI.E_61-80.dim.0.en: Answers questions immediately without pausing and expects the same from others.

EI.E_61-80.dim.1.en: Prefers spending time in large groups, has a vast number of acquaintances and many friends.

EI.E_61-80.dim.2.en: If a situation is unsatisfactory, will take vigorous action to change it and try to draw everyone's attention to it.

EI.E_61-80.dim.3.en: Many people see them as an uninhibited, confident person with no complexes.

EI.E_61-80.dim.4.en: Easily picks up a conversation after being interrupted, and often interrupts others or takes phone calls in the middle of a discussion.

EI.E_61-80.dim.5.en: Enjoys meetings, attends all of them, sometimes initiates them, frequently speaks up, and gains an emotional boost from them.

EI.E_61-80.dim.6.en: Regularly shares their thoughts and ideas with those around them.

EI.E_61-80.dim.7.en: Takes part in all conversations, talks a great deal, and often initiates discussions themselves.

EI.E_61-80.dim.8.en: Thinks out loud, naturally drawing listeners in.

EI.E_61-80.dim.9.en: Appreciates frequent praise from managers, colleagues, and subordinates, especially when given verbally.

EI.E_41-60.dim.0.en: Responds to questions very quickly and prefers others to reply promptly as well.

EI.E_41-60.dim.1.en: Enjoys spending time in large social groups and has many friends.

EI.E_41-60.dim.2.en: When dissatisfied with a situation, actively seeks to change it.

EI.E_41-60.dim.3.en: Some people perceive them as uninhibited and free from self-consciousness.

EI.E_41-60.dim.4.en: Does not notice being interrupted during a conversation and may easily switch to an incoming phone call.

EI.E_41-60.dim.5.en: Enjoys frequent meetings, often speaks up at them, and gains energy from the experience.

EI.E_41-60.dim.6.en: Regularly shares their thoughts and ideas with those around them.

EI.E_41-60.dim.7.en: Prefers talking over listening in conversations and often initiates discussions.

EI.E_41-60.dim.8.en: Tends to think out loud, verbalizing thoughts as a way of processing them.

EI.E_41-60.dim.9.en: Enjoys public praise, especially when it is given verbally.

EI.E_21-40.dim.0.en: Responds quickly and dislikes having to wait long for replies from others.

EI.E_21-40.dim.1.en: Enjoys spending time in social settings, knows many people, and has an active circle of friends.

EI.E_21-40.dim.2.en: If a situation is unsatisfactory and feels it can be changed, will make an effort to change it.

EI.E_21-40.dim.3.en: Some people perceive them as an open and uninhibited person.

EI.E_21-40.dim.4.en: Does not mind being interrupted during a conversation, and will occasionally interrupt others themselves.

EI.E_21-40.dim.5.en: Tries not to miss group meetings, sometimes speaks up at them, and tends to leave in a better mood.

EI.E_21-40.dim.6.en: Readily shares their thoughts and ideas with those around them.

EI.E_21-40.dim.7.en: Can play both speaker and listener in a conversation, though more often takes the speaking role and sometimes initiates discussions.

EI.E_21-40.dim.8.en: May think a task through by talking it out loud to make it easier to handle.

EI.E_21-40.dim.9.en: Praise, especially public recognition, serves as a strong source of motivation.

EI.E_1-20.dim.1.en: Enjoys spending time with others and has a few acquaintances and friends.

EI.E_1-20.dim.2.en: Does not hide dissatisfaction when a situation does not suit them.

EI.E_1-20.dim.3.en: Tends to behave in a relaxed rather than reserved manner.

EI.E_1-20.dim.4.en: If interrupted during a conversation, can usually pick up where they left off with ease.

EI.E_1-20.dim.5.en: Attends important meetings but may skip less significant ones, and occasionally speaks up.

EI.E_1-20.dim.6.en: Sometimes shares their ideas and thoughts with those around them.

EI.E_1-20.dim.7.en: Speaks calmly and moderately during conversations, mostly stays on topic, listens attentively, and occasionally initiates discussion.

EI.E_1-20.dim.8.en: When faced with a difficult task, may think it through by speaking it aloud.

EI.E_1-20.dim.9.en: Does not mind receiving public recognition for their work.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.0.en: Usually responds to questions quickly, but may take a moment to think when the question is complex.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.1.en: Prefers spending time in small to medium-sized groups and maintains a moderate number of close friendships.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.2.en: Whether to change a situation or adapt to it depends on how dissatisfied they feel with current circumstances.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.3.en: Their level of openness or reserve varies depending on the situation.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.4.en: In engaging conversations, they try not to interrupt the other person.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.5.en: Feels comfortable at meetings and may speak up on their own initiative.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.6.en: Willing to share thoughts and ideas with trusted people in their inner circle.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.7.en: Takes on the role of speaker or listener in a conversation based on the circumstances.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.8.en: Usually thinks things through internally, but may also think out loud or write ideas down.

EI.balanced_0-0.dim.9.en: Appreciates praise that aligns with their own assessment of their work.

EI.I_1-20.dim.0.en: Thinks through a response before giving it.

EI.I_1-20.dim.1.en: Prefers small groups with few acquaintances and close friends.

EI.I_1-20.dim.2.en: If a situation is unsatisfactory but not overly inconvenient, tends not to show it.

EI.I_1-20.dim.4.en: Generally dislikes being interrupted and avoids interrupting others.

EI.I_1-20.dim.5.en: Attends only mandatory meetings and rarely speaks up at them.

EI.I_1-20.dim.6.en: Not particularly inclined to share ideas and thoughts with others.

EI.I_1-20.dim.7.en: Can take on a speaker role if asked, but generally prefers to listen.

EI.I_1-20.dim.8.en: Prefers to think things through mentally, sometimes in writing.

EI.I_1-20.dim.9.en: Does not place much trust in compliments.

EI.I_21-40.dim.0.en: Often pauses before responding and does not rush others to reply.

EI.I_21-40.dim.1.en: When social interaction is required, prefers small gatherings and keeps a limited circle of friends.

EI.I_21-40.dim.2.en: If a situation is unsatisfactory but change seems unlikely, tends not to attempt altering it.

EI.I_21-40.dim.3.en: Some people perceive them as shy and reflective.

EI.I_21-40.dim.4.en: Dislikes being interrupted and allows others to finish speaking before responding.

EI.I_21-40.dim.5.en: Avoids meetings when possible, needs time to recharge emotionally afterward, and speaks up only when asked.

EI.I_21-40.dim.6.en: Finds it difficult to share thoughts and ideas with those around them.

EI.I_21-40.dim.7.en: Typically takes on the role of listener in conversations and rarely initiates them.

EI.I_21-40.dim.8.en: Needs to concentrate in order to think clearly; external noise is distracting.

EI.I_21-40.dim.9.en: Approaches people who give compliments with caution and some skepticism.

EI.I_41-60.dim.0.en: Will always pause before responding and may ask the other person to think before answering as well.

EI.I_41-60.dim.1.en: Dislikes spending time in social gatherings and has few close friends.

EI.I_41-60.dim.2.en: When a situation is uncomfortable, tends to adapt to it rather than change it.

EI.I_41-60.dim.3.en: Some people perceive them as withdrawn or shy.

EI.I_41-60.dim.4.en: Dislikes being interrupted while speaking and does not interrupt others; will decline incoming calls during a conversation.

EI.I_41-60.dim.5.en: Dislikes meetings, feels emotionally drained by them, rarely speaks up, and needs rest afterward.

EI.I_41-60.dim.6.en: Shares thoughts and ideas with others only when directly asked.

EI.I_41-60.dim.7.en: Prefers listening over talking in conversations and rarely initiates them.

EI.I_41-60.dim.8.en: Prefers silence and solitude when thinking things through.

EI.I_41-60.dim.9.en: Is skeptical of people who are overly generous with compliments.

EI.I_61-80.dim.0.en: Always pauses before responding and expects the same from others.

EI.I_61-80.dim.1.en: Prefers spending time alone over any social gathering; has very few close friends.

EI.I_61-80.dim.2.en: If a situation displeases them, they won't show it and will instead try to adjust their own attitude toward it.

EI.I_61-80.dim.3.en: Many people perceive them as withdrawn, shy, or even secretive.

EI.I_61-80.dim.4.en: When speaking, they expect not to be interrupted and never interrupt others; gets irritated if the other person is distracted by an incoming call.

EI.I_61-80.dim.5.en: Avoids meetings, sees them as a waste of time, does not speak up in them, and needs solitude and rest afterward.

EI.I_61-80.dim.6.en: Rarely shares their thoughts and ideas with those around them.

EI.I_61-80.dim.7.en: If there is any opportunity to avoid joining a conversation, they will take it and will never initiate one themselves.

EI.I_61-80.dim.8.en: During periods of reflection, they can withdraw into themselves for quite a long time.

EI.I_61-80.dim.9.en: Receiving an excessive number of compliments makes them uneasy and suspicious of the person giving them.

EI.I_81-100.dim.0.en: Takes a long time to formulate responses and tends to view quick answers from others as inherently incorrect.

EI.I_81-100.dim.1.en: Actively avoids spending time in social groups and has very few friends or none at all.

EI.I_81-100.dim.2.en: When dissatisfied with a situation, never speaks up or shows it, and instead tries hard to adapt.

EI.I_81-100.dim.3.en: Most people perceive them as withdrawn, shy, and even secretive.

EI.I_81-100.dim.4.en: If interrupted during a conversation, may feel offended and stop engaging; often puts their phone on silent during discussions.

EI.I_81-100.dim.5.en: Strongly avoids meetings, which drain and exhaust them emotionally; significant recovery time is needed afterward.

EI.I_81-100.dim.6.en: Avoids sharing their thoughts and ideas, preferring to keep everything to themselves.

EI.I_81-100.dim.7.en: Does their best to avoid participating in conversations; when forced to engage, takes on the role of a listener only.

EI.I_81-100.dim.8.en: Avoids group brainstorming; during periods of reflection, disconnects completely from the outside world, and those around them often struggle to understand their need for solitude.

EI.I_81-100.dim.9.en: Dislikes small talk, is skeptical of compliments, and has no tolerance for flattery.

SN.dimName.3.en: Quantity of tasks vs. quality of execution

SN.dimName.4.en: Facts and figures vs. theories and ideas

SN.dimName.7.en: Completing tasks as a whole vs. step by step

SN.S_81-100.dim.0.en: Tends to overload answers and questions with excessive detail, often obscuring the main point.

SN.S_81-100.dim.1.en: Jumps straight into a task without prior reflection, which can result in poor quality outcomes.

SN.S_81-100.dim.2.en: Only engages with tactical tasks and completely disregards anything strategic.

SN.S_81-100.dim.3.en: Prioritizes completing more tasks faster rather than focusing on quality.

SN.S_81-100.dim.4.en: Only interested in present-moment facts and figures; ideas, theories, and future planning hold no appeal.

SN.S_81-100.dim.5.en: Overly meticulous when working with documents, often falling into perfectionism when drafting them.

SN.S_81-100.dim.6.en: Will not take action without explicit instructions, rules, or clear directives.

SN.S_81-100.dim.7.en: Becomes deeply absorbed in task details, frequently losing sight of the overall picture.

SN.S_81-100.dim.8.en: Maintains a near-fanatical dedication to order and tidiness in the workplace.

SN.S_81-100.dim.9.en: Interprets everything in strictly literal terms, rejecting the possibility of alternative meanings.

SN.S_61-80.dim.0.en: Often provides more detail than the question requires, going beyond what is needed.

SN.S_61-80.dim.1.en: When given a task, tends to act on it immediately without always thinking it through first.

SN.S_61-80.dim.2.en: Readily takes on tactical tasks, sometimes at the expense of strategic ones.

SN.S_61-80.dim.3.en: Prioritizes the quantity of tasks completed over their quality.

SN.S_61-80.dim.4.en: More interested in facts and figures, sometimes at the expense of theories and ideas.

SN.S_61-80.dim.5.en: When working with documents, tries to understand every point in detail, sometimes losing sight of the bigger picture.

SN.S_61-80.dim.6.en: When receiving a task, requires clear and detailed instructions.

SN.S_61-80.dim.7.en: When completing a task, becomes deeply absorbed in the details, sometimes losing sight of the overall objective.

SN.S_61-80.dim.8.en: Maintains a perfect order at their workspace and expects the same from colleagues.

SN.S_61-80.dim.9.en: Takes most things literally, including situations that may carry a deeper or figurative meaning.

SN.S_41-60.dim.0.en: Prefers precise answers to precise questions.

SN.S_41-60.dim.1.en: Focuses on present-moment tasks, favoring action over reflection.

SN.S_41-60.dim.2.en: Prefers short, tactical tasks with tangible outcomes.

SN.S_41-60.dim.3.en: Takes on many tasks and strives to complete them well, though the quality of some may suffer.

SN.S_41-60.dim.4.en: Favors facts and figures over ideas and theories.

SN.S_41-60.dim.5.en: Reads documents from beginning to end.

SN.S_41-60.dim.6.en: Becomes frustrated when clear instructions are not provided.

SN.S_41-60.dim.7.en: Grasps the details of a task more readily than the overall picture.

SN.S_41-60.dim.8.en: Dislikes clutter and disorder in the workspace.

SN.S_21-40.dim.0.en: When answering questions, tends to break responses down into clear points or bullet items.

SN.S_21-40.dim.1.en: Prefers to dive into tasks without spending too much time thinking them through beforehand.

SN.S_21-40.dim.2.en: Favors short-term tasks over long-term ones when given the choice.

SN.S_21-40.dim.3.en: Aims to complete many tasks quickly, though the quality of some may occasionally suffer.

SN.S_21-40.dim.4.en: Relies more on facts and figures than on theories or abstract ideas when making decisions.

SN.S_21-40.dim.5.en: Makes an effort to read documents received in their entirety.

SN.S_21-40.dim.6.en: Prefers tasks that come with clear and specific instructions.

SN.S_21-40.dim.7.en: May show a slight loss of overall perspective on a task while maintaining strong attention to its details.

SN.S_21-40.dim.8.en: Tends to tidy up their workspace before starting to work on tasks.

SN.S_21-40.dim.9.en: Inclined to take things literally, though occasionally may attempt to look for a deeper or secondary meaning.

SN.S_1-20.dim.0.en: When answering a question, often asks for additional details before responding.

SN.S_1-20.dim.1.en: Prefers to start tasks quickly, after a brief period of reflection.

SN.S_1-20.dim.2.en: More interested in short-term tasks than long-term ones.

SN.S_1-20.dim.3.en: Generally completes tasks on time and with satisfactory quality.

SN.S_1-20.dim.4.en: When choosing between theory and practice, tends to rely on facts and figures.

SN.S_1-20.dim.5.en: When time allows, reads documents thoroughly from start to finish.

SN.S_1-20.dim.6.en: Treats received instructions as a direct guide to action.

SN.S_1-20.dim.7.en: Pays close attention to the specific details of a task.

SN.S_1-20.dim.8.en: Regularly tidies and organizes their workspace.

SN.S_1-20.dim.9.en: Tends to interpret things literally rather than abstractly.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.0.en: Adjusts the precision of responses to match the precision of the questions asked.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.1.en: Strives to maintain a balance between thinking through tasks and actually executing them.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.2.en: Has no preference between short-term and long-term tasks.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.3.en: Keeps a balance between the quantity and quality of completed work.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.4.en: Can draw on facts and figures or theories and ideas depending on the situation.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.5.en: Reviews documents to the extent that is necessary and sufficient.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.6.en: Can work effectively both in ambiguous conditions and under clear, structured instructions.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.7.en: Gives equal attention to the overall vision of a task and to its specific details.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.8.en: Neutral attitude toward order and organization in the workplace.

SN.balanced_0-0.dim.9.en: Whether thinking in abstract or literal terms depends on the context of the situation.

SN.N_1-20.dim.0.en: May omit details in responses that they consider unimportant.

SN.N_1-20.dim.1.en: Tends to think through a task before beginning to work on it.

SN.N_1-20.dim.2.en: More interested in long-term or extended tasks.

SN.N_1-20.dim.3.en: Strives to complete tasks well and generally meets deadlines.

SN.N_1-20.dim.4.en: When choosing between practice and theory, ideas and theories tend to win out.

SN.N_1-20.dim.5.en: Reads the most important sections of documents thoroughly, while trying to grasp the general meaning of the rest.

SN.N_1-20.dim.6.en: If they see that a task could be done better, they may deviate from the instructions given.

SN.N_1-20.dim.7.en: Having a view of the task as a whole is important to them.

SN.N_1-20.dim.9.en: Tends to interpret things in a figurative or abstract way.

SN.N_21-40.dim.0.en: Does not require a high degree of precision in questions or answers.

SN.N_21-40.dim.1.en: Thinks through assigned tasks before acting, sometimes missing deadlines as a result.

SN.N_21-40.dim.2.en: Prefers long-term tasks over short-term ones.

SN.N_21-40.dim.3.en: Strives to complete tasks perfectly, sometimes at the expense of other responsibilities.

SN.N_21-40.dim.4.en: Relies more on theories and ideas than on facts and figures when making decisions.

SN.N_21-40.dim.5.en: When reading documents, tries to grasp the main idea without getting bogged down in details.

SN.N_21-40.dim.6.en: When choosing tasks, prefers those that allow greater freedom of action.

SN.N_21-40.dim.7.en: May overlook certain details of a task while still maintaining a clear overall vision.

SN.N_21-40.dim.8.en: Tends to tidy up their workspace only when there are no interesting tasks to focus on.

SN.N_21-40.dim.9.en: Tries to find a deeper meaning in most things.

SN.N_41-60.dim.0.en: Prefers asking and answering questions in broad, general terms.

SN.N_41-60.dim.1.en: More inclined to think things through than to take immediate action.

SN.N_41-60.dim.2.en: Favors long-term, strategic tasks with overarching outcomes.

SN.N_41-60.dim.3.en: Pursues perfection in some tasks while largely ignoring others.

SN.N_41-60.dim.4.en: Finds ideas and theories more engaging than present-day facts.

SN.N_41-60.dim.5.en: Tends to delegate paperwork and documentation to others.

SN.N_41-60.dim.6.en: Comfortable working in ambiguous or uncertain conditions.

SN.N_41-60.dim.7.en: Grasps the overall picture more readily than individual details.

SN.N_41-60.dim.8.en: Tolerates workplace disorder without distress when absorbed in meaningful work.

SN.N_41-60.dim.9.en: Consistently seeks deeper meaning and purpose in everything.

SN.N_61-80.dim.0.en: Often gives a broader answer than the question requires, drifting into general topics.

SN.N_61-80.dim.1.en: Before completing a task, tends to think it through extensively, which can lead to delays.

SN.N_61-80.dim.2.en: Eagerly takes on strategic tasks, sometimes at the expense of tactical ones.

SN.N_61-80.dim.3.en: May become fixated on perfecting one thing while neglecting other tasks.

SN.N_61-80.dim.4.en: Ideas and theories are more appealing than facts and figures, often to their detriment.

SN.N_61-80.dim.5.en: When working with documents, tries to grasp the overall meaning rather than diving into details.

SN.N_61-80.dim.6.en: When carrying out tasks, often disregards given instructions in favor of freedom of action.

SN.N_61-80.dim.7.en: Maintains a clear vision of the task at hand, sometimes overlooking important details.

SN.N_61-80.dim.8.en: Frequently fails to notice disorder in their own workspace.

SN.N_61-80.dim.9.en: Tends to search for deeper meaning in everything, sometimes wasting time on things that are straightforward.

SN.N_81-100.dim.0.en: Getting a clear answer is quite difficult, as they tend to drift into vague and general statements.

SN.N_81-100.dim.1.en: When given a task, they may discuss it endlessly without ever actually starting it.

SN.N_81-100.dim.2.en: Only strategic tasks hold their interest; tactical or operational tasks are largely ignored.

SN.N_81-100.dim.3.en: While perfecting one task, they tend to lose track of everything else.

SN.N_81-100.dim.4.en: Drawn to ideas and theories bordering on philosophy; has little interest in what is happening in the present moment.

SN.N_81-100.dim.5.en: Dislikes working with documents, often delegating reading to others and asking for a summary of the key points.

SN.N_81-100.dim.6.en: Resistant to instructions and rules, preferring complete freedom of action.

SN.N_81-100.dim.7.en: Looks at the task as a whole and never digs into the details.

SN.N_81-100.dim.8.en: Completely unaware of the disorder in their own workspace.

SN.N_81-100.dim.9.en: Seeks deeper meaning in everything, rejecting the idea that some things are simply literal.

TF.dimName.5.en: Willingness to go back on one's promises

TF.dimName.8.en: Strength of position (take back / not take back one's word)

TF.T_81-100.dim.0.en: Remains completely calm even in the most critical situations; some people believe they have no emotions at all.

TF.T_81-100.dim.1.en: Achieves goals through arguments, facts, and proving a point rather than persuasion or emotional appeal; many perceive them as authoritarian.

TF.T_81-100.dim.2.en: Extremely objective in their decisions; some people find them cold and indifferent.

TF.T_81-100.dim.3.en: Exceptional organizational skills; in the flow of work processes, there is often little room left for social interaction.

TF.T_81-100.dim.4.en: Prone to excessive self-reliance and heroics; never asks for help and is supremely confident in their own abilities.

TF.T_81-100.dim.5.en: If they give their word, they will keep it no matter what sacrifices it requires.

TF.T_81-100.dim.6.en: In reports and reviews, focuses on unfinished tasks and mistakes — their own and others' — and never praises anyone.

TF.T_81-100.dim.7.en: Makes difficult decisions without any emotional involvement; some people see them as a machine.

TF.T_81-100.dim.8.en: Never takes back what they have said, regardless of how many people it offended or how deeply.

TF.T_81-100.dim.9.en: Readily engages in conflict; some people consider them excessively harsh.

TF.T_61-80.dim.0.en: Makes decisions without emotional interference in critical situations.

TF.T_61-80.dim.1.en: Achieves results through arguments, facts, and logical reasoning; dislikes persuading others through emotional appeals.

TF.T_61-80.dim.2.en: It is nearly impossible to influence the objectivity of their assessments and decisions.

TF.T_61-80.dim.3.en: Excellent organizational skills; optimally structures their own workflow and that of colleagues and subordinates.

TF.T_61-80.dim.4.en: Always self-confident and strives to handle everything independently.

TF.T_61-80.dim.5.en: Follows through on commitments; feels genuine distress if unable to deliver.

TF.T_61-80.dim.6.en: Frames reports around the future: what can still be done, what mistakes can be corrected, and how to improve.

TF.T_61-80.dim.7.en: Capable of making tough decisions and struggles to understand why others cannot do the same.

TF.T_61-80.dim.8.en: Will not take back words, even if they have offended someone.

TF.T_61-80.dim.9.en: Willingly engages in conflict if they believe it will accelerate the completion of a task.

TF.T_41-60.dim.0.en: Able to remain calm and composed in situations where others lose their self-control.

TF.T_41-60.dim.1.en: Prefers to get their way through arguments, facts, proving a point, and debate.

TF.T_41-60.dim.2.en: Makes objective decisions and takes pride in doing so.

TF.T_41-60.dim.3.en: Better developed organizational skills than social communication abilities.

TF.T_41-60.dim.4.en: Does everything themselves and uses this to demonstrate their value to others.

TF.T_41-60.dim.5.en: Tries to keep their word; and if unable to do so, says so in advance to avoid letting people down.

TF.T_41-60.dim.6.en: In reports, focuses on what was not completed, and on their own and others' mistakes and shortcomings.

TF.T_41-60.dim.8.en: Will not take back what they have said.

TF.T_41-60.dim.9.en: Will readily engage in conflict if they believe that conflict will be beneficial to the cause.

TF.T_21-40.dim.0.en: Remains fairly calm in critical situations.

TF.T_21-40.dim.1.en: Finds it much easier to get results through arguments and facts than through emotions or persuasion.

TF.T_21-40.dim.2.en: Their assessments and decisions are largely objective.

TF.T_21-40.dim.3.en: Prefers organizing work tasks over planning social events.

TF.T_21-40.dim.4.en: Tends to handle things independently and trusts themselves more than others.

TF.T_21-40.dim.5.en: Makes a point of only promising what they can actually deliver.

TF.T_21-40.dim.6.en: Focuses reports on future possibilities and opportunities.

TF.T_21-40.dim.7.en: Does not struggle significantly with making difficult decisions.

TF.T_21-40.dim.8.en: It is hard to imagine them taking back something they have said.

TF.T_21-40.dim.9.en: Is willing to engage in conflict if they believe it will benefit the outcome.

TF.T_1-20.dim.0.en: Usually remains calm in critical situations.

TF.T_1-20.dim.1.en: Can achieve more through arguments and facts than through emotions and persuasion.

TF.T_1-20.dim.2.en: Their assessments and decisions tend to be objective.

TF.T_1-20.dim.3.en: Would find more satisfaction in planning a workday than in casual morning coffee chats with colleagues.

TF.T_1-20.dim.4.en: When faced with the choice of doing something alone or asking for help, tends to handle it independently.

TF.T_1-20.dim.5.en: Generally makes an effort to follow through on promises.

TF.T_1-20.dim.6.en: In reports, reflects reality and touches briefly on future possibilities.

TF.T_1-20.dim.7.en: Can make difficult decisions when necessary.

TF.T_1-20.dim.8.en: May take back words only if they have seriously hurt someone.

TF.T_1-20.dim.9.en: Potential conflicts do not intimidate them.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.0.en: Experiences some emotions in critical situations, but generally remains calm.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.1.en: Combines logical arguments with emotional appeal to achieve goals.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.2.en: Judgments and decisions can be either objective or subjective depending on the situation.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.3.en: Balances planning the workday with casual social interaction, such as chatting with colleagues over morning coffee.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.4.en: Handles simple tasks independently, but may bring in help for more complex ones.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.5.en: May occasionally fail to follow through on overpromises, but generally keeps commitments.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.6.en: Typically reflects reality accurately in reports.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.7.en: Will take responsibility for a difficult decision when there is no one else to delegate it to.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.8.en: May retract words if they have seriously hurt someone close.

TF.balanced_0-0.dim.9.en: Generally dislikes conflict, but is willing to engage in it if no other way to resolve the issue is apparent.

TF.F_1-20.dim.0.en: Critical situations tend to cause noticeable anxiety.

TF.F_1-20.dim.1.en: Often achieves more through emotional appeal than through logic and facts.

TF.F_1-20.dim.2.en: Their assessments and decisions tend to be subjective rather than objective.

TF.F_1-20.dim.3.en: Enjoys casual morning coffee chats with colleagues more than planning out the workday.

TF.F_1-20.dim.4.en: When faced with a task, tends to ask for help rather than tackle it alone.

TF.F_1-20.dim.5.en: Occasionally makes promises that turn out to be difficult or impossible to keep.

TF.F_1-20.dim.6.en: In reports, focuses on what has already been accomplished and includes a personal touch.

TF.F_1-20.dim.7.en: Tends to avoid making difficult decisions when there is an opportunity to do so.

TF.F_1-20.dim.8.en: May retract statements if they realize those words hurt someone.

TF.F_1-20.dim.9.en: Feels uncomfortable and unsettled in conflict situations.

TF.F_21-40.dim.0.en: Cannot remain calm and composed in critical situations.

TF.F_21-40.dim.1.en: Finds it much easier to get what they want through emotions and persuasion than through arguments and facts.

TF.F_21-40.dim.2.en: Their assessments and decisions tend to be subjective.

TF.F_21-40.dim.3.en: Would much rather organize a party than coordinate work tasks.

TF.F_21-40.dim.4.en: Often enlists the help of others, trusting those around them more than themselves.

TF.F_21-40.dim.5.en: Sometimes makes promises not to keep them, but to maintain a reputation as a good person.

TF.F_21-40.dim.6.en: In reports, emphasizes achieved results and those who stood out.

TF.F_21-40.dim.7.en: Tends to delegate difficult decisions to others.

TF.F_21-40.dim.8.en: Will most likely take back words that have hurt or offended someone.

TF.F_21-40.dim.9.en: Tries to avoid taking on tasks that may involve conflict during their execution.

TF.F_41-60.dim.0.en: Does not hide anxiety or distress in critical situations.

TF.F_41-60.dim.1.en: Tends to get what they want through emotional appeal, persuasion, and manipulation.

TF.F_41-60.dim.2.en: Their decisions are often subjective, taking into account the feelings and interests of those around them.

TF.F_41-60.dim.3.en: Social communication skills are more developed than organizational or leadership abilities.

TF.F_41-60.dim.4.en: Has a keen sense of when others need them and uses this awareness to their advantage.

TF.F_41-60.dim.5.en: Maintaining a good relationship matters more than being strictly honest, so they often promise what is expected rather than what they can actually deliver.

TF.F_41-60.dim.6.en: Skilled at accounting for their actions and writing reports in a way that praises everyone, especially themselves.

TF.F_41-60.dim.7.en: Finds it difficult to make tough or uncomfortable decisions.

TF.F_41-60.dim.8.en: Will retract statements if they turn out to have hurt or offended someone.

TF.F_41-60.dim.9.en: Strongly prefers harmony and actively tries to avoid conflict.

TF.F_61-80.dim.1.en: Achieves a great deal through persuasion and emotional influence; dislikes arguing, presenting facts, or proving a point.

TF.F_61-80.dim.2.en: It is nearly impossible to sway their subjective judgments and decisions.

TF.F_61-80.dim.3.en: Excellent social skills; resolves issues for colleagues, subordinates, and management; often serves as the emotional hub of the team.

TF.F_61-80.dim.4.en: More confident in others than in themselves; consistently seeks to involve helpers in tasks.

TF.F_61-80.dim.5.en: Treats their own words as expressions of intent rather than firm commitments, and therefore often fails to follow through on promises.

TF.F_61-80.dim.6.en: Frames reports around the past: what was accomplished and who stood out; praises everyone, but primarily themselves.

TF.F_61-80.dim.7.en: Struggles to make difficult decisions and finds it hard to understand how others do so easily.

TF.F_61-80.dim.8.en: Will immediately retract a statement if it has offended someone.

TF.F_61-80.dim.9.en: Will go out of their way to resolve a conflict, even if doing so slows down the completion of a task.

TF.F_81-100.dim.0.en: Highly emotional in critical situations; some people see them as prone to panic.

TF.F_81-100.dim.1.en: Achieves goals through persuasion and emotional influence rather than logic, arguments, or debate; many people perceive them as manipulative.

TF.F_81-100.dim.2.en: Extremely subjective in decision-making, prioritizing the feelings of those affected; some people view them as unfair.

TF.F_81-100.dim.3.en: Exceptional social skills; time spent engaging with colleagues, subordinates, and superiors may leave little room for actual work.

TF.F_81-100.dim.4.en: May take advantage of relationships, manipulating others' feelings and emotions to get their work done.

TF.F_81-100.dim.5.en: Frequently breaks promises, seeing themselves not as dishonest but as a careful diplomat.

TF.F_81-100.dim.6.en: Reports tend to focus exclusively on praising everyone's achievements, including their own, while ignoring mistakes.

TF.F_81-100.dim.7.en: Tends to avoid making difficult decisions; some people consider them hypersensitive.

TF.F_81-100.dim.8.en: Will immediately retract words they believe may have offended someone; some people see them as excessively accommodating.

TF.F_81-100.dim.9.en: Avoids conflict at all costs and will never be the cause of one; some people may perceive them as lacking willpower.

JP.dimName.2.en: Working by Plan or Free Spirit

JP.J_81-100.dim.0.en: Maintains extreme order in everything — belongings, documents, tasks; there is no room for spontaneity in life; many people see him as a pedant.

JP.J_81-100.dim.1.en: Keeps emotions fully under control and cannot be thrown off balance; some people feel he has no emotions at all.

JP.J_81-100.dim.2.en: Appears to have every moment of life planned out; any disruption to the plan causes significant distress.

JP.J_81-100.dim.3.en: Tries to complete almost everything immediately, sometimes without thinking it through properly.

JP.J_81-100.dim.4.en: Expresses thoughts with maximum clarity so everyone understands; stays firmly on topic, though may occasionally miss new ideas along the way.

JP.J_81-100.dim.5.en: Extremely methodical and calculating, avoids risk, thinks several steps ahead; some people find him boring.

JP.J_81-100.dim.6.en: Always sees tasks through to completion, even knowing they may soon need to be redone; some people consider him a workaholic.

JP.J_81-100.dim.7.en: Always arrives on time and becomes noticeably irritated when others are late; some people find his punctuality excessive.

JP.J_81-100.dim.8.en: Views work as a duty and obligation and does not understand people who treat it as entertainment or pleasure.

JP.J_81-100.dim.9.en: A highly stable member of society and a cornerstone of the community; some people consider him overly conservative.

JP.J_61-80.dim.0.en: Maintains clear order in everything — belongings, documents, tasks; others may perceive this need for organization as obsessive.

JP.J_61-80.dim.1.en: Keeps emotions well under control; needs a compelling reason to shift mood; can work effectively in any physical or emotional state.

JP.J_61-80.dim.2.en: Plans work well in advance and dislikes deviating from the plan; if something unplanned gets done, they will add it to the list just to cross it off.

JP.J_61-80.dim.3.en: Tackles assigned tasks immediately without delay.

JP.J_61-80.dim.4.en: Expresses thoughts clearly and sequentially, stays on topic, and is difficult to lead off on a tangent during conversation.

JP.J_61-80.dim.5.en: Actively seeks to eliminate risk from their affairs; may sometimes take longer than necessary to reach a decision.

JP.J_61-80.dim.6.en: Finishes current tasks before starting new ones; prefers not to have many things in progress at the same time.

JP.J_61-80.dim.7.en: Always punctual and regularly ends up waiting for others who are habitually late.

JP.J_61-80.dim.8.en: Views work as a duty and obligation; tends to be suspicious of people who treat work as a form of entertainment.

JP.J_61-80.dim.9.en: Conservative by nature; favors traditional values, follows established rules, and expects the same from others.

JP.J_41-60.dim.0.en: Maintains order in everything: belongings, documents, and tasks.

JP.J_41-60.dim.1.en: Emotionally stable; needs a strong reason to shift mood; can work through physical and emotional discomfort.

JP.J_41-60.dim.2.en: Plans work in advance and dislikes deviating from the plan.

JP.J_41-60.dim.3.en: Handles tasks immediately rather than putting them off until later.

JP.J_41-60.dim.4.en: Expresses thoughts clearly and sequentially, stays on topic, and is difficult to distract.

JP.J_41-60.dim.5.en: Consistent and calculated in actions, always thinking about consequences.

JP.J_41-60.dim.6.en: Sees every task through to completion; will not start something new if the current task can be finished first.

JP.J_41-60.dim.7.en: Always arrives on time and does not understand why others might be late.

JP.J_41-60.dim.8.en: Views work as a duty; lack of enjoyment from a job is not a sufficient reason to leave it.

JP.J_41-60.dim.9.en: Sees themselves as part of a system; unlikely to frequently change jobs or careers; socially stable; respects authority; follows the rules; thrives in large teams.

JP.J_21-40.dim.0.en: Generally keeps things, documents, and tasks well organized.

JP.J_21-40.dim.1.en: Usually emotionally stable; maintains consistent productivity throughout the workday.

JP.J_21-40.dim.2.en: Prefers working according to a plan, which enhances personal productivity.

JP.J_21-40.dim.3.en: Tends not to delay or postpone assigned tasks.

JP.J_21-40.dim.4.en: Typically expresses thoughts in a logical sequence and rarely loses track of a conversation.

JP.J_21-40.dim.5.en: Views risk in work and business as an obstacle rather than an opportunity.

JP.J_21-40.dim.6.en: Prefers finishing tasks over starting new ones.

JP.J_21-40.dim.7.en: Almost always arrives on time and considers being late a personal shortcoming.

JP.J_21-40.dim.8.en: Approaches work as a duty and responsibility; personal enjoyment is secondary.

JP.J_21-40.dim.9.en: Rarely changes careers and prefers steady, sequential professional development; tends to thrive in larger organizations.

JP.J_1-20.dim.0.en: When given a choice between tidying up and creative work, will choose to bring order.

JP.J_1-20.dim.1.en: Mood remains relatively stable throughout the workday and does not significantly affect productivity.

JP.J_1-20.dim.2.en: When given a choice between working to a plan or freely, prefers to follow a plan.

JP.J_1-20.dim.3.en: Typically gets started on a task right away after receiving an assignment.

JP.J_1-20.dim.4.en: Once beginning to express a thought, tends to see it through to completion.

JP.J_1-20.dim.6.en: Finds satisfaction in completing tasks and finishing what was started.

JP.J_1-20.dim.7.en: Feels uneasy about arriving late or at the wrong time.

JP.J_1-20.dim.8.en: Views work first as a responsibility, and only then as a source of enjoyment.

JP.J_1-20.dim.9.en: Inclined to follow established rules and develop professionally at a steady, gradual pace.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.0.en: Comfortable with order but can also function in mildly chaotic environments.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.1.en: Does not experience significant mood shifts throughout the day.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.2.en: Able to work both according to a plan and in a flexible, open-ended manner.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.3.en: Takes a reasonable pause before beginning an assigned task.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.4.en: Generally presents thoughts in a logical sequence, though may occasionally lose the thread.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.6.en: Equally comfortable starting and finishing tasks.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.7.en: Usually arrives on time, but may occasionally be late.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.8.en: Balances duty and enjoyment in their work.

JP.balanced_0-0.dim.9.en: Functions well in both large and small teams.

JP.P_1-20.dim.0.en: When faced with a choice between tidying up and doing creative work, will opt for the creative task.

JP.P_1-20.dim.1.en: Mood may shift during the workday without any obvious reason, though it rarely has a significant impact on productivity.

JP.P_1-20.dim.2.en: When given the option to work according to a plan or freely, tends to prefer a flexible, unstructured approach.

JP.P_1-20.dim.3.en: Does not rush to start a task immediately after receiving an assignment.

JP.P_1-20.dim.4.en: When expressing a thought, does not always follow it through to a conclusion.

JP.P_1-20.dim.5.en: Is capable of taking calculated risks when the situation calls for it.

JP.P_1-20.dim.6.en: Finds genuine enjoyment in starting new projects and initiatives.

JP.P_1-20.dim.7.en: Does not feel particularly troubled about arriving late or off schedule.

JP.P_1-20.dim.8.en: Approaches work as a source of pleasure first and an obligation second.

JP.P_1-20.dim.9.en: Tends toward innovation and may change jobs or careers more frequently than others.

JP.P_21-40.dim.0.en: Believes that rigid structure can negatively impact their creative abilities.

JP.P_21-40.dim.1.en: Emotional state and productivity tend to fluctuate throughout the workday.

JP.P_21-40.dim.2.en: Dislikes working according to a plan, as it feels limiting to their freedom of action.

JP.P_21-40.dim.3.en: Tends to procrastinate on assigned tasks.

JP.P_21-40.dim.4.en: Usually expresses thoughts in a non-linear but engaging way.

JP.P_21-40.dim.5.en: Believes that taking risks is necessary to achieve success.

JP.P_21-40.dim.6.en: Prefers starting new projects over seeing them through to completion.

JP.P_21-40.dim.7.en: Often arrives late and does not consider this a personal flaw.

JP.P_21-40.dim.8.en: Perceives work as a source of enjoyment; duty is a secondary concern.

JP.P_21-40.dim.9.en: Frequently changes professions, prefers to develop in leaps, enjoys being outside of systems, and favors small teams.

JP.P_41-60.dim.0.en: Does not believe order is everything; has nothing against structure, but values creativity, spontaneity, and quick reactions above all.

JP.P_41-60.dim.1.en: Mood can shift several times a day; works productively during emotional highs and struggles during low periods.

JP.P_41-60.dim.2.en: Does not plan work in advance, preferring to wait and see what actually needs to be done.

JP.P_41-60.dim.3.en: Often gets things done at the last minute.

JP.P_41-60.dim.4.en: Expresses thoughts in a non-linear way, may switch topics, and is easily thrown off track.

JP.P_41-60.dim.5.en: Spontaneous and willing to take risks without worrying too much about the consequences.

JP.P_41-60.dim.6.en: Loves starting projects but dislikes seeing them through to completion.

JP.P_41-60.dim.7.en: Often arrives late to meetings and does not see punctuality as essential to getting things done.

JP.P_41-60.dim.8.en: Treats work as play; if a job stops being enjoyable, tends to move on.

JP.P_41-60.dim.9.en: Sees themselves as outside the system; may search for a deeper calling; often changes careers; a natural innovator and inventor who thrives in small teams.

JP.P_61-80.dim.0.en: Their workspace, papers, and belongings tend to be creatively disorganized; they prioritize a creative approach, spontaneity, and quick reactions over orderliness.

JP.P_61-80.dim.1.en: Their mood shifts frequently, and their productivity follows suit; during peak periods, sudden insights and breakthroughs are common.

JP.P_61-80.dim.2.en: Working according to a strict plan feels restrictive; they prefer to do whatever feels most necessary or meaningful in the moment.

JP.P_61-80.dim.3.en: They sometimes miss deadlines because they postpone tasks, waiting for a second wind or a burst of motivation.

JP.P_61-80.dim.4.en: They often change the subject mid-conversation; new thoughts and ideas easily distract them and pull their attention away.

JP.P_61-80.dim.5.en: They frequently take risks in their endeavors, not always with sufficient justification.

JP.P_61-80.dim.6.en: They tend to start new projects before finishing old ones, and may have many tasks running simultaneously.

JP.P_61-80.dim.7.en: They are often late and others frequently wait for them; they genuinely do not understand why punctuality matters so much to people, as they see it as unrelated to actual success.

JP.P_61-80.dim.8.en: They approach work as play and a source of enjoyment; they are unlikely to engage with tasks they find uninteresting.

JP.P_61-80.dim.9.en: They are natural innovators who explore, experiment, and eagerly try new things, often breaking established rules and systems.

JP.P_81-100.dim.0.en: Order is sacrificed for creativity; some people perceive them as chaotic and inconsistent.

JP.P_81-100.dim.1.en: Mood shifts several times a day; during bursts of creative energy they can be exceptionally productive, while during low periods they may be unable to work at all.

JP.P_81-100.dim.2.en: Lives in the moment and acts according to circumstances; plans and schedules feel oppressive; some people see them as chaotic.

JP.P_81-100.dim.3.en: Tries to complete almost everything at the last minute; often succeeds, but it drives everyone else around them crazy.

JP.P_81-100.dim.4.en: Frequently goes off on tangents in conversation, jumping from topic to topic; can become distracted at any moment, yet rarely misses new thoughts and ideas.

JP.P_81-100.dim.5.en: Extremely risk-prone and thrill-seeking; willing to put everything on the line; some consider them irresponsible.

JP.P_81-100.dim.6.en: Often leaves tasks unfinished because new and interesting things come along; some people view them as scattered.

JP.P_81-100.dim.7.en: Frequently late; becomes noticeably irritated when others point it out; genuinely believes that discipline limits their creative freedom; many people consider them unpunctual.

JP.P_81-100.dim.8.en: Views work purely as an enjoyable game; the moment it stops being fun, they will not hesitate to move on.

JP.P_81-100.dim.9.en: An innovator, inventor, and revolutionary; breaks existing systems in order to build something new; some people consider them extremely unconventional.
